purpose: Compact proof-candidate map for evaluating app/tool/interactive builds as support for a new service offer. Use this to decide what belongs in the service system and what should remain out of scope for now.

candidates.label: Piko artist website + interactive DJ mixer
strengths: blends website and interactive product thinking
shows custom front-end and experience design ability
can demonstrate a sellable service beyond brochure sites
risks: must be framed clearly so it does not feel like random creative experimentation
may need tighter articulation of buyer problem and project purpose
candidates.bestUse: Primary proof candidate for a custom tools / apps / interactive products service.

candidates.label: Guitar app
strengths: supports a stronger app/tool capability story
useful if the flow, UX, and purpose are coherent
risks: could read as hobby work if not tied to a clear product or buyer problem
needs maturity and feature clarity to support a sellable service
candidates.bestUse: Supporting or primary proof candidate depending on maturity and framing.

candidates.label: Pro studio mixer
strengths: could strongly reinforce interactive product capability
aligned with custom tool/product build positioning if mature
risks: should not be included if the build is incomplete or visually weak
could duplicate what Piko already proves unless it offers clearer product depth
candidates.bestUse: Hold until strong enough to support the offer cleanly.

candidates.label: Blazing Star Therapy website
risks: not the right primary proof for a tools/apps service
fits website strategy and trust-led service work more strongly
candidates.bestUse: Keep for website strategy / trust-driven client proof, not this service expansion.

selectionRule.primaryRecommendation: Lead with one strongest proof candidate, not multiple equal examples.
selectionRule.supportRecommendation: Use a second build only if it adds range or product depth.
selectionRule.guardrail: If a project cannot be framed around a buyer problem and a sellable outcome, do not surface it yet.
